core package

Submodules

core.admin module

Admin registrations for publishers, articles, and newsletters.

class core.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Expose article moderation fields in the Django admin interface.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'created_at')
list_filter = ('approved', 'publisher')
property media
prepopulated_fields = {'slug': ('title',)}
search_fields = ('title', 'summary', 'content')
class core.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Configure newsletter editing and filtering in the admin site.

list_display = ('subject', 'author', 'publisher', 'created_at')
list_filter = ('publisher',)
property media
search_fields = ('subject', 'body')
class core.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

Display publishers in the Django admin with search support.

list_display = ('name', 'slug')
property media
search_fields = ('name', 'slug')

core.apps module

Application configuration for the core publishing app.

class core.apps.CoreConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

Register the core app with Django.

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
name = 'core'

core.forms module

Forms for publishers, article drafts, and newsletter editing.

Create or edit newsroom articles.

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class core.forms.PublisherScopedFormMixin(*args, user=None, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: object

Restrict publisher choices to the publishers a user may work with.

get_publisher_queryset()[source]

Return the publishers that should be visible in the current form.

core.models module

Core data models for publishers, articles, and newsletters.

class core.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Store an article draft or approved publication for the newsroom.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

approved

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

approved_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id
content

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_absolute_url()[source]

Return the canonical detail page for the current article.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_next_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)

get_previous_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

class core.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Represent a publisher that editors and journalists can join.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

description

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editors

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

journalists

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

name

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

newsletters

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
save(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Populate the slug automatically before saving a publisher.

slug

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

subscribed_readers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

core.models.build_unique_slug(model_class, value, instance=None)[source]

Generate a slug that is unique for the given model and value.

core.services module

Service helpers for article approval and reader notifications.

core.services.approve_article(article, editor=None)[source]

Mark an article as approved and trigger subscriber notifications.

core.services.collect_article_subscribers(article)[source]

Return readers who follow the article’s author or publisher.

core.services.post_article_to_x(article)[source]

Placeholder for optional social media publishing.

core.services.send_article_approval_email(article)[source]

Notify subscribed readers when an article is approved.

core.tests module

Regression tests for publisher workflows and editorial permissions.

class core.tests.EditorialPermissionsTests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Verify that editors can manage content created by journalists.

setUp()[source]

Create newsroom fixtures used by the editorial permission tests.

test_editor_can_delete_another_users_article()[source]

Editors should be able to delete articles they did not author.

test_editor_can_delete_another_users_newsletter()[source]

Editors should be able to delete newsletter drafts by journalists.

test_editor_can_update_another_users_article()[source]

Editors should be able to edit articles created by journalists.

test_editor_can_update_another_users_newsletter()[source]

Editors should be able to update newsletter drafts by journalists.

class core.tests.PublisherWorkflowTests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Verify publisher creation and membership flows for newsroom users.

setUp()[source]

Create editor and journalist accounts used by workflow tests.

test_editor_can_create_publisher_and_is_added_to_it()[source]

The publisher creator should automatically join as an editor.

test_journalist_can_join_a_publisher()[source]

Journalists should be able to join existing publishers.

core.urls module

URL routes for the public site, publishing tools, and moderation pages.

core.views module

Views that power the public site, publishing tools, and review queue.

class core.views.ApproveArticleView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, EditorRequiredMixin, View

Approve a pending article and trigger downstream notifications.

post(request, pk)[source]

Approve the selected article and return the editor to the review queue.

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, JournalistRequiredMixin, CreateView

Create a new article draft for the authenticated journalist.

form_class

alias of ArticleForm

form_valid(form)[source]

Attach the current user as author before saving the article.

get_form_kwargs()[source]

Pass the current user into the form for publisher scoping.

model

alias of Article

template_name = 'core/article_form.html'

Delete the article and show a success message.

get_context_data(**kwargs)[source]

Describe the article being deleted in the shared confirmation template.

model

alias of Article

success_url = '/articles/'
template_name = 'core/confirm_delete.html'

Bases: DetailView

Display an article while honoring role-based visibility rules.

context_object_name = 'article'

Expose edit, delete, and approve permissions to the template.

get_queryset()[source]

Allow editors to see all drafts and journalists to see their own.

model

alias of Article

template_name = 'core/article_detail.html'
class core.views.ArticleEditorAccessMixin[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, EditorOrJournalistRequiredMixin

Allow editors to access all articles and journalists to access their own.

get_queryset()[source]

Restrict journalists to their own articles while editors see all.

Bases: RoleRequiredMixin

Allow access to either editors or journalists.

class core.views.PublisherMembershipToggleView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, EditorOrJournalistRequiredMixin, View

Join or leave a publisher as either an editor or journalist.

get_membership_manager(publisher)[source]

Return the correct many-to-many manager for the user’s role.

post(request, pk)[source]

Toggle the current user’s membership in the selected publisher.

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, EditorRequiredMixin, ListView

List unapproved articles that still need editorial review.

context_object_name = 'articles'
get_queryset()[source]

Return the pending article queue ordered by newest first.

model

alias of Article

template_name = 'core/article_review_list.html'
class core.views.RoleRequiredMixin[source]

Bases: UserPassesTestMixin

Restrict a view to signed-in users with one of the required roles.

handle_no_permission()[source]

Redirect signed-in users to the dashboard with an error message.

required_roles = ()
test_func()[source]

Return True when the current user satisfies the role check.

Module contents

Core publishing models, forms, views, and services for the News App.
